title: triticum spontaneum f. garniense
intro: Triticum spontaneum f. garniense, also known as wild garlic or bear garlic, is a subspecies of the wild hardy perennial plant belonging to the wheat grass family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of wild garlic include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving respiratory health, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of wild garlic include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
